# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 1070
- **Lines of code**: 345,590
- **Languages**: TypeScript (902), CSS (44), JavaScript (41), Markdown (32), JSON (27)
- **Zones**: 34 (34 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-08T17:45:01.016Z

## Architecture Zones

### Hench Agent (`hench-agent`)

The autonomous agent package responsible for task selection, LLM-driven execution loops, tool dispatch, and run recording for n-dx's self-directed development mode.

- Files: 155
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-agent/context.md`

### Monorepo E2E Tests (`monorepo-e2e-tests`)

End-to-end CLI contract, delegation, error, and architecture-policy tests that exercise the full ndx orchestration layer as a black box.

- Files: 21
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/monorepo-e2e-tests/context.md`

### Monorepo Integration Tests (`monorepo-integration-tests`)

Validates cross-package API contracts and scheduler startup behavior at the monorepo boundary, independent of any single package's test suite.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/monorepo-integration-tests/context.md`

### Prd Analysis Engine (`packages-rex:prd-analysis-engine`)

The PRD analysis and proposal pipeline: scans project files, deduplicates and decomposes items, validates structure, proposes new PRD entries, and exposes the analyze and reshape CLI commands.

- Files: 73
- Cohesion: 0.63, Coupling: 0.37

- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex-prd-analysis-engine/context.md`

### Prd Cli Operations (`packages-rex:prd-cli-operations`)

CLI command handlers for PRD mutation and reporting (status, update, move, remove, adapter) together with the shared output formatting, error handling, and core lifecycle utilities (timestamps, stats, parent-completion) they depend on.

- Files: 37
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54

- Cohesion: 0.36, Coupling: 0.64

### Prd Schema Foundation (`packages-rex:prd-schema-foundation`)

The shared foundational layer of the rex package: schema type definitions, tree traversal primitives, core domain operations (DAG, transitions, cascade-reset), and the file-store adapter that all other zones depend on.

- Files: 32
- Cohesion: 0.31, Coupling: 0.69
- Entry points: packages/rex/src/analyze/acknowledge.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/add.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/recommend.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/report.ts, packages/rex/src/core/cascade-reset.ts (+9 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex-prd-schema-foundation/context.md`

### Remote Sync Adapters (`packages-rex:remote-sync-adapters`)

Manages remote synchronization with third-party project trackers (Jira, Notion) through a registry-based adapter pattern, including integration schemas and canonical ID mapping.

- Files: 18
- Cohesion: 0.39, Coupling: 0.61
- Entry points: packages/rex/src/cli/commands/sync.ts, packages/rex/src/core/canonical.ts, packages/rex/src/store/adapter-registry.ts, packages/rex/src/store/index.ts, packages/rex/src/store/integration-schema.ts (+4 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex-remote-sync-adapters/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit
GET     /api/hench/concurrency
GET     /api/hench/config
GET     /api/hench/execute/status
GET     /api/hench/execute/status/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory
GET     /api/hench/memory/history
GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory/leaks
GET     /api/hench/metrics
GET     /api/hench/metrics/snapshots
GET     /api/hench/runs
GET     /api/hench/runs/:id
GET     /api/hench/runs/health
GET     /api/hench/task-usage
GET     /api/hench/templates
GET     /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/throttle
POST    /api/hench/execute
POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
POST    /api/hench/runs/:id/mark-stuck
POST    /api/hench/templates
POST    /api/hench/templates/:id/apply
POST    /api/hench/throttle/emergency-stop
POST    /api/hench/throttle/pause
POST    /api/hench/throttle/resume
PUT     /api/hench/config
PUT     /api/hench/throttle
```

```
POST    /api/hench/workflow/suggestions/:id
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- hench has no internal module boundary enforcement: 155 files across 31 directories share a flat import namespace with no sub-domain barrels or path restrictions. Cross-subdomain imports (e.g. agent/tools importing store internals directly) cannot be detected by any current lint or test rule. Without explicit internal boundaries, the zone risks accumulating the same coupling entropy that produced the web-build-infrastructure artifact. [info] (hench-agent)
- architecture-policy.test.js is a single point of failure for four-tier hierarchy enforcement. If this file is skipped, broken, or omitted from a CI run, cross-tier import violations can merge undetected. The policy should be backed by at least one redundant mechanism (e.g. an eslint import boundary rule or a dedicated CI step that runs before package tests). [critical] (monorepo-e2e-tests)
- Only 2 integration test files exist at the monorepo boundary. As cross-package interactions grow (rex↔sourcevision data flow, hench↔rex task selection, web↔rex MCP contract), the integration ring has no mechanism to enforce proportional growth — unlike the e2e suite which has an architecture-policy guard. A test coverage policy for cross-package contracts is absent. [warning] (monorepo-integration-tests)
- Reference documents (CODEX.md, PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, ENFORCEMENT.md) are co-located with executable orchestration scripts (cli.js, web.js, ci.js) at the repo root. This conflates two distinct concerns — documentation artifacts and runnable entry points — making it harder to audit the orchestration surface at a glance. Move reference docs to docs/ to make the root a clean entry-point manifest. [info] (monorepo-root)
- The two-phase pending-proposals.json → acknowledged-findings.json workflow has no crash-recovery sentinel. A tool run that terminates between the two writes leaves both files in an inconsistent state with no machine-detectable signal. This is a data-integrity gap that could cause silent PRD corruption on restart. [warning] (rex-runtime-state)
- shared-types.ts defines types consumed cross-zone by web-dashboard but lives inside the analytics zone with no explicit export contract. Consumers reach into zone-internal types rather than through a stable interface boundary, making the contract implicit and fragile if the zone is ever refactored. [warning] (task-usage-analytics)
- The phantom zone's 0.73 coupling score is being counted in aggregate web-package health metrics. Any CI gate or automated report that sums or averages zone coupling will treat the web package as unhealthy due solely to a community-detection artifact, not a real architectural problem. This false signal may cause teams to ignore legitimate future coupling regressions because the baseline is already flagged. [warning] (web-build-infrastructure)
- Zone mixes Node.js server code and browser Preact viewer code in a single zone. These two sub-environments are mutually exclusive at runtime — Node modules (http, fs) cannot load in the browser and vice versa. A split into web-server and web-viewer sub-zones would enforce the runtime boundary structurally, not just by convention. [warning] (web-dashboard)
- God function: cmdPrune in packages/rex/src/cli/commands/prune.ts calls 34 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- architecture-policy.test.js is the only zone in the entire codebase that enforces the four-tier dependency hierarchy as a runtime assertion — it is a single point of failure for import-boundary enforcement; consider whether additional policy checks (e.g. gateway module purity) should live here [info] (monorepo-e2e-tests)
- Zero coupling is actively enforced by the spawn-only architecture of cli.js and web.js — any future change that introduces a direct `import` from a domain package into these scripts would immediately break the isolation guarantee and should be treated as a critical regression. [info] (monorepo-root)
- The data-layer contract (never imported at runtime) is convention-only. Consider adding a lint rule or CI check that asserts no `.ts`/`.js` source file contains an import path resolving into `.rex/` to make the contract machine-enforceable rather than doc-only. [warning] (rex-runtime-state)
- The cleanup scheduler (usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts) is a stateful process-level concern but is absent from monorepo-integration-tests — if the scheduler interacts with rex or sourcevision stores at startup, that contract is currently untested at the integration boundary [warning] (task-usage-analytics)

### Relationships

- The rex-gateway.ts boundary module is the sole interface between hench-agent and the rex domain — this single-gateway pattern means any future rex API change has exactly one file to update in hench, and any unintended direct import from rex would be immediately visible in a diff. This is the cleanest cross-package interface pattern in the codebase. [info] (hench-agent)
- scheduler-startup.test.js in monorepo-integration-tests partially overlaps the concern of task-usage-analytics scheduler tests — confirm there is no test duplication and that the integration test validates cross-package startup ordering rather than internal scheduler logic [info] (monorepo-integration-tests)
- docs/architecture/ files are referenced from CLAUDE.md but have no automated freshness check. If architectural decisions change (e.g. the four-tier hierarchy evolves or gateway rules shift), these documents will silently become stale without any tooling signal. [warning] (project-documentation)
- The co-location of packages/hench/src/store/suggestions.ts with web package files in this zone creates a false import-graph edge between the hench and web tiers. Any zone-coupling metric that includes this zone will over-report cross-tier coupling until suggestions.ts is reassigned to hench-agent. [warning] (web-build-infrastructure)
- The single usage→web-dashboard import is the only dependency that flows against the expected data-consumer direction (viewer should pull from analytics, not the reverse); verify this import is not a hidden circular initialization dependency [warning] (web-dashboard)
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 79 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- Bidirectional coupling: "task-usage-analytics" ↔ "web-dashboard" (1+3 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 383 files vs smallest with 2 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Fan-in hotspot: packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts receives calls from 24 files — high-impact module, changes may have wide ripple effects [warning]
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (hench-agent)
- At 155 files, hench is the largest zone in this batch; consider whether internal sub-domains (agent loop, tool dispatch, store, PRD client) are clearly delineated by directory structure to aid navigation. [info] (hench-agent)
- Cohesion 1.0 and coupling 0.0 confirm hench is architecturally well-isolated — all cross-package dependencies flow through the rex-gateway.ts boundary module as intended. [info] (hench-agent)
- The adaptive analysis module (agent/analysis/adaptive.ts) alongside review.ts and spin.ts suggests a multi-strategy analysis pipeline — ensure these strategies are tested independently so regressions are localizable. [info] (hench-agent)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (monorepo-e2e-tests)
- The breadth of CLI command coverage (ci, config, dev, plan, start, status, sync, usage, work) means this zone is the primary regression barrier for the orchestration tier — keeping tests fast and deterministic is essential. [info] (monorepo-e2e-tests)
- Zero coupling across 21 test files confirms the E2E suite treats the CLI as a true black box, which maximises its value as a refactoring safety net. [info] (monorepo-e2e-tests)
- ... and 30 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "hench-agent" has files across 31 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (hench-agent)
- Define a maximum-scope policy for rex-gateway.ts: document which categories of rex API are in-scope for re-export (task selection, store read/write) and which are explicitly out-of-scope. Without this, the gateway will grow to mirror the entire rex public API as new hench features are added, defeating its purpose as a narrow boundary. [warning] (hench-agent)
- Extend the barrel-per-subdomain pattern (already used in agent/analysis/) to agent/tools, store, and prd sub-directories. Consistent barrel files would make the internal boundary surface explicit and prevent cross-subdomain imports from accumulating silently. [info] (hench-agent)
- Zone "web-build-infrastructure" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (web-build-infrastructure)
- Dissolving this zone is a prerequisite for any coupling trend analysis to be meaningful across the web package — the 0.73 coupling score conflates two independent architectural signals (hench-store misclassification and viewer-component placement) into one metric that cannot be acted on without disambiguation. [warning] (web-build-infrastructure)
- packages/web/src/landing/index.html should be explicitly reclassified to the web-viewer zone (as the app shell that bootstraps viewer components) when the phantom zone is dissolved, not left as an unclaimed file. Its current placement in build-infrastructure incorrectly implies it is a build artifact rather than a runtime entry point. [info] (web-build-infrastructure)
- Zone "Web Build Infrastructure" (web-build-infrastructure) has catastrophic risk (score: 0.73, cohesion: 0.27, coupling: 0.73) — requires immediate architectural intervention [critical] (web-build-infrastructure)
- Zone "web-dashboard" has files across 23 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (web-dashboard)
- Zone name 'web-dashboard' does not reflect its actual content (server, viewer, MCP routes, CLI, schema). Rename to 'web-platform' or split into 'web-server' / 'web-viewer' to make the name a truthful description of the zone's scope. [info] (web-dashboard)
- concurrent-execution-metrics.ts name implies it tracks hench agent execution concurrency. Verify this file does not import from the hench package — such an import would violate the four-tier hierarchy (web is domain tier, hench is execution tier, domain must not import from execution). [warning] (web-dashboard)
- ... and 42 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
